creator: Lucas Ropek
title: (No AI) Apple plans to make iOS 27 a Choose Your Own Adventure of AI models
dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
content: With Apple's latest operating system updates, users will reportedly have their pick of which third-party AI models they want to use for a host of tasks.
contentSnippet: With Apple's latest operating system updates, users will reportedly have their pick of which third-party AI models they want to use for a host of tasks.

creator: Connie Loizos
title: (No AI) ASML CEO Christophe Fouquet on his company’s monopoly: no one is coming for us
dc:creator: Connie Loizos
content: Christophe Fouquet, who became ASML's CEO in 2024 after more than a decade at the company, sat down with this editor on the rooftop deck of his Beverly Hills hotel Tuesday morning ahead of his appearance at the Milken Institute Global Conference. Dressed in a blue suit and white shirt, he was relaxed — even when the conversation turned to the rivals.

title: (No AI) Microsoft gives up on Xbox Copilot AI
author: Jay Peters
content:
![Promo image showing Xbox Copilot AI on mobile screens around a TV](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/05/01_Wire-Hero-1920x1080-1-0b305b4a9ace7898196b.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Xbox is "winding down Copilot on mobile" and "will stop development of Copilot on console," new Xbox CEO Asha Sharma announced on Tuesday. The move follows [Sharma's reorganization](https://www.theverge.com/news/923908/microsoft-xbox-reorg-platform-changes) of the Xbox platform team earlier on Tuesday, which added executives from Microsoft's CoreAI team - where Sharma worked before taking over Xbox - to the Xbox side of the company.

[Sharma, on X](https://x.com/asha_shar/status/2051746410660593933?s=46):

> Xbox needs to move faster, deepen our connection with the community, and address friction for both players and developers.
> 
> Today, we promoted leaders who helped build Xbox, while also bringing in new voices to help push us forward. This balance is important as we get th …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/games/924551/microsoft-xbox-ceo-copilot-ai-asha-sharma)
contentSnippet:
Xbox is "winding down Copilot on mobile" and "will stop development of Copilot on console," new Xbox CEO Asha Sharma announced on Tuesday. The move follows Sharma's reorganization of the Xbox platform team earlier on Tuesday, which added executives from Microsoft's CoreAI team - where Sharma worked before taking over Xbox - to the Xbox side of the company.
Sharma, on X:
Xbox needs to move faster, deepen our connection with the community, and address friction for both players and developers.
Today, we promoted leaders who helped build Xbox, while also bringing in new voices to help push us forward. This balance is important as we get th …

Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Xbox is "winding down Copilot on mobile" and "will stop development of Copilot on console," new Xbox CEO Asha Sharma announced on Tuesday. The move follows Sharma's reorganization of the Xbox platform team earlier on Tuesday, which added executives from Microsoft's CoreAI team - where Sharma worked before taking over Xbox - to the Xbox […]

title: (No AI) Apple could let you pick a favorite AI model in iOS 27
author: Stevie Bonifield
content:
![A hands-on photo of Apple’s iPhone 16E.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2025/02/DSC_2327.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

The next update to Apple's operating systems could allow users to choose their preferred AI model for running Apple Intelligence. According to [_Bloomberg_](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-05-05/ios-27-features-apple-plans-to-let-users-swap-models-across-apple-intelligence)'s Mark Gurman, Apple is planning to allow third-party chatbots to power its AI features system-wide in iOS 27, iPadOS 27, and macOS 27, all expected for this fall. In addition to [running Siri](https://www.theverge.com/tech/902048/apple-siri-ai-chatbot-update-ios-27), compatible third-party AI models, called "Extensions," will also now be able to run other Apple Intelligence features like Writing Tools and Image Playground.

According to Gurman, Apple will also allow users to choose different Siri voices for different AI models - Siri responses from one of Apple's …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/924515/apple-intelligence-third-party-chatbot-extensions-ios-27)

According to Bloomberg's Mark Gurman, Apple is planning to allow third-party chatbots to power its AI features system-wide in iOS 27, iPadOS 27, and macOS 27, all expected for this fall. In addition to running Siri, compatible third-party AI models, called "Extensions," will also now be able to run other Apple Intelligence features like Writing Tools and Image Playground. 
According to Gurman, Apple will also allow users to choose different Siri voices for different AI models - Siri responses from one of Apple's  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

In addition to running […]

creator: Todd Feathers
title: (No AI) He Couldn’t Land a Job Interview. Was AI to Blame?
dc:creator: Todd Feathers
content: Armed with some Python and a white-hot sense of injustice, one medical student spent six months trying to figure out whether an algorithm trashed his job application.
contentSnippet: Armed with some Python and a white-hot sense of injustice, one medical student spent six months trying to figure out whether an algorithm trashed his job application.
